channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: The games that show the flaws in a 48-team World Cup
item.description: Two matches in the final round of group games present the chance for two teams to simply play out a draw to qualify.

item.title: Why it's time for Saka and Rashford - Shearer
item.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains why Thomas Tuchel needs to change some personnel as well as his team's mindset when they face Panama.

item.title: Putellas decides to join London City Lionesses
item.description: Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas decides to move to London City Lionesses after leaving Barcelona.

item.title: Stay or go home? Tartan Army in limbo as Scotland await fate
item.description: BBC Scotland examines options for the Tartan Army in Miami as Scotland wait to discover if their World Cup adventure will continue or not.

item.title: Bellamy to stay with Wales as Burnley move off
item.description: Wales manager Craig Bellamy's proposed move to take charge at Championship side Burnley has fallen through.

item.title: What Scotland need to reach World Cup knockouts
item.description: After their 3-0 defeat by Brazil, BBC Sport looks at what Scotland require to reach the knockout phase.

item.title: Hincapie completes permanent £34.5m Arsenal move
item.description: Defender Piero Hincapie makes his loan move from Bayer Leverkusen to Arsenal permanent.

item.title: Burnley and West Ham to meet on first Championship weekend
item.description: Relegated Burnley and West Ham will meet on the opening weekend of the Championship season on Sunday, 16 August.

item.title: World Cup makes more than enough money - New York mayor Mamdani
item.description: Mayor of New York Zohran Mamdani says the World Cup generates "more than enough" income and supporters should not have to pay higher prices.

item.title: New Hearts boss Vrancken ready to challenge Old Firm
item.description: Hearts appoint former Genk and Sint-Truiden boss Wouter Vrancken as their new head coach a week after Derek McInnes left for Rangers.

item.title: Scotland on the brink after defeat by Brazil
item.description: Scotland's hopes of reaching the knockout stages of the World Cup for the first time are in significant peril with calamitous defending allowed Brazil to win 3-0 in Miami and finish top of Group C.

item.title: Championship fixtures: Your club-by-club guide
item.description: See your Championship club's full fixture list for the forthcoming 2026-27 season.

item.title: Leicester start League One season at Notts County
item.description: Leicester City will start their League One season at promoted Notts County, while Bromley's first-ever match in the division will be at Barnsley.

item.title: League One fixtures: Your club-by-club guide
item.description: The League One fixtures for the 2026-27 season have been released - find your club's full list here.

item.title: York host Bristol Rovers and Rochdale visit Newport
item.description: Promoted York City host Bristol Rovers in their first League Two game of the season after a decade away from the EFL.

item.title: League Two fixtures: Your club-by-club guide
item.description: The League Two fixtures for the 2026-27 season have been released and here is where you can find links to your team's full fixture list.

item.title: Morocco progress after victory over Haiti
item.description: Soufiane Rahimi and Gessime Yassine score late goals for Morocco to deny Haiti their first-ever World Cup point in Atlanta.

item.title: South Africa into round of 32 after win over South Korea
item.description: Thapelo Maseko's second-half goal gives South Africa a 1-0 win over South Korea in Monterrey, and sends them into the World Cup knockout phase.

item.title: Vinicius Jr pounces on Scotland mistake to give Brazil early lead
item.description: Brazil forward Vinicius Junior pounces on a mistake by the Scotland defence to give his side an early lead in their World Cup Group C match in Miami.

item.title: 'A phenomenal goal!' Isidor hits incredible strike for Haiti
item.description: Wilson Isidor gives Haiti a lead against Morocco with an "extraordinary" World Cup goal at the Atlanta Stadium.

item.title: Mexico beat Czech Republic to maintain 100% winning record
item.description: Mexico score three second-half goals to eliminate the Czech Republic from the World Cup and end the group phase with maximum points.

item.title: Tottenham sign former Arsenal midfielder Pelova
item.description: Tottenham confirm the signing of Netherlands midfielder Victoria Pelova following her exit from Arsenal.

item.title: How brands banned from the World Cup became the story
item.description: When we try to suppress something, we often make it more visible - and Fifa is living that in real time with its sponsors.

item.title: French footballer Kies, 21, dies from drowning
item.description: French footballer Kenzo Kies has died at the age of 21 after drowning in the Rhone river.

item.title: Pochettino has US believing they can be World Cup winners
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino has united the USA fans and players in a belief that they are capable of winning the World Cup.

item.title: Best World Cup stadium? BBC Sport experts have their say
item.description: BBC Sport reporters have their say on the best stadium experiences at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Holders Luton drawn with Peterborough in EFL Trophy
item.description: The draw for the 2026-2027 EFL Trophy is made, with 16 invited Premier League U21 sides joining League One and League Two clubs.

item.title: Belgium going out? Sutton's World Cup 2026 score predictions - final group games
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the final set of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: Vinicius Jr stops fun and leaves Scots down... but are they out?
item.description: Scotland's party stops at the hands of Brazil in Miami, but might they still find themselves in the last 32?

item.title: Neymar - the return of Brazil's forgotten hero
item.description: BBC Scotland's Scott Mullen delves into the return of Brazil's Neymar against Scotland.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 18
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: 'Think we're going home' - Clarke & McGinn rue Scotland mistakes
item.description: Scotland will have to wait until potentially the early hours of Sunday to find out if their World Cup campaign will continue, but midfielder John McGinn suspects that is "unlikely" and head coach Steve Clarke suspects they are "going home".

item.title: The Gakpo conundrum facing Liverpool
item.description: Cody Gakpo is thriving with the Netherlands but increased competition means his Liverpool future is uncertain.

item.title: BBC and Sidemen to host World Cup watchalong
item.description: BBC Sport and YouTube collective the Sidemen team up for a World Cup watchalong on Thursday.

item.title: Godfrey signs new three-year deal with London City
item.description: England international Freya Godfrey has signed a new three-year contract with London City Lionesses.

item.title: Bosnia boost last-32 hopes after win over Qatar
item.description: Bosnia-Herzegovina boost their hopes of reaching the knockout stages in the World Cup for a first ever time defeating Qatar in their final Group B fixture.

item.title: Switzerland beat Canada but both through to last 32
item.description: Switzerland seal their place in the first knockout round of the World Cup by beating co-hosts Canada to top Group B.

item.title: David scores with first touch for Canada
item.description: Canada forward Promise David scores with his first touch to give his side hope in their Group B match against Switzerland in Vancouver.

item.title: 'What a moment!' Alajbegovic scores stunning opener for Bosnia
item.description: 18-year-old winger Kerim Alajbegovic scores a superb opening goal for Bosnia in their World Cup Group B match against Qatar in Seattle.

item.title: England held to goalless draw by Ghana
item.description: England fail to repeat their performance that overpowered Croatia in their first World Cup game as they are held to a draw by a resolute Ghana at Boston Stadium.

item.title: Colombia qualify for last 32 with win over DR Congo
item.description: Daniel Munoz's deflected strike is enough for Colombia to beat DR Congo and secure their place in the 2026 World Cup knockout stage.

item.title: Croatia earn narrow win to knock out Panama
item.description: Croatia remain in contention for a last-32 spot at the World Cup after earning their first points in Group L with a win over Panama, who are knocked out of the World Cup following the loss.

item.title: Scotland keeper Gunn on Man Utd radar - Thursday's gossip
item.description: Scotland goalkeeper Angus Gunn could be a target for Manchester United, while Arsenal continue to pursue Aston Villa midfielder Morgan Rogers and Everton make a move for West Ham captain Jarrod Bowen, plus more.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: 'Mixed emotions' as Canada qualify but lose home advantage
item.description: Losing their final group game to Switzerland means co-hosts Canada will play no further matches in their home country at this year's World Cup.

item.title: What each team need to reach World Cup knockouts
item.description: With the final round of group fixtures under way the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape.

item.title: Spurs want West Ham's Fernandes after Tonali bid rejected
item.description: West Ham value Mateus Fernandes at £80m amid interest from Tottenham, who want a central midfielder this summer and have had a bid rejected for Newcastle's Sandro Tonali.

item.title: England's James and Rice to have fitness assessed
item.description: England will assess the fitness of Reece James and Declan Rice before their final World Cup group game against Panama.

item.title: Tuchel's England are opposite to Southgate's - and built to beat top teams
item.description: BBC Sport's football tactics writer Umir Irfan explains why Thomas Tuchel's England system is built to prosper against the world's top nations.

item.title: World Cup's best matches, moments, goals & players so far - ranked
item.description: After two rounds of fixtures, BBC Sport ranks the best matches, moments, players and goals of the World Cup so far.

item.title: As Messi turns 39, how he and Ronaldo keep breaking records
item.description: Lionel Messi, 39, and Cristiano Ronaldo, 41, keep delivering on the world's biggest stage.

item.title: Leeds agree contract with Fulham forward Wilson
item.description: Leeds agree a long-term contract with Fulham attacker Harry Wilson to join on a free transfer.

item.title: Quiz: How well do you know birthday boy Messi at World Cups?
item.description: Argentina great Lionel Messi turns 39 today and has been lighting up the World Cup this summer, but how well do you know his journey so far?

item.title: The beauty of sharing your child's first World Cup
item.description: There is nothing like the first World Cup you can enjoy with your kid - and the wide-eyed wonder through which they see it.

item.title: No need to panic for England - but this was a reality check
item.description: England's drab goalless draw with Ghana is no cause to panic - but it does serve as a reality check, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: Why do some World Cup shirts keep tearing during matches?
item.description: Puma shirts have torn four times during the World Cup so far, so what exactly is going on?

item.title: Miami romance, Billy Gilmour and a mince and tattie hot dog
item.description: Another city. Another ballpark. Another Tartan Army love-in. The warmth towards the Scotland fans during this World Cup burns hotter than the Florida sun.

item.title: The World Cup stars being targeted by Premier League clubs
item.description: The World Cup stars being targeted by Premier League clubs. Who has caught the eye already, and which clubs are focusing on longer-term priorities?

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is BBC's 3D Live Match Experience and how does it work?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the unique way of following matches during the 2026 World Cup via the BBC

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?
item.description: The opening match of the 2026 World Cup was dominated by players wearing pink boots - but why is the colour so prevalent in football at the moment?

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: 'Dream come true' - McCrorie returns to Rangers from Bristol City
item.description: Ross McCrorie is reuniting with manager Derek McInnes and returning to Rangers after Bristol City accept an undisclosed offer from the Ibrox club.

item.title: Who is new Hearts head coach Vrancken?
item.description: BBC Sport Scotland charts the life and career of Belgian head coach Wouter Vrancken, who is taking over at Hearts.

item.title: What we learned from McInnes' Rangers unveiling
item.description: BBC Sport Scotland lays out what we learned from Derek McInnes' first press conference as Rangers manager.

item.title: Bologna open talks for Rangers' Raskin - gossip
item.description: Rangers midfielder attracts interest from Italy and England and Scotland goalkeeper could land move to Premier League giants...

item.title: Lazio to pay compensation in pregnancy dispute case
item.description: Lazio Women have been ordered to pay compensation to former midfielder Maja Gothberg because they unlawfully ended her employment due to pregnancy.

item.title: Brighton in advanced talks to sign Walti
item.description: Brighton are in advanced talks to sign Juventus and Switzerland midfielder Lia Walti.

item.title: Real sign Schroeder in 'most expensive' women's transfer
item.description: BK Hacken teenager Felicia Schroeder has joined Real Madrid in a deal that makes the 19-year-old forward "the most expensive transfer ever in women's football".

item.title: Phelan named permanent Everton manager
item.description: Scott Phelan has been appointed permanent head coach of Everton Women following an interim period last season.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
